_comment: Golden queries template for neuralmind benchmark --quality. Copy this to tests/fixtures/benchmark_queries_custom.json and register the suite in evals/quality/runner.py. See tests/benchmark/golden_queries.yaml for a fully-documented 40-query reference example.

queries.question: How does JWT authentication work in this codebase?
Where is the database connection pool configured?
How do we handle rate limiting on the /orders endpoint?
A user login is failing without any error message — which file contains credential validation?
I need to add an email_verified column to users — which files would change?
I just changed how JWT tokens are signed — what other files should I review?

_usage.step_1: Write 20-30 queries based on real questions your team asks each week. Favor questions where getting the wrong file would waste time — auth, billing, core data models.
_usage.step_2: Set expected_modules to the file paths (no line numbers) that must appear in the top-5 results. Two or three files per cross-file query; one for focused queries.
_usage.step_3: Set shape: 'focused' (one expected module), 'cross-file' (2-3 modules), or 'identity' (exact-symbol lookup like a function name).
_usage.step_4: Set category to one of: 'architecture' (how does X work?), 'bug-localization' (where is the bug?), 'refactor' (what files change?), or 'next-edit' (what else should I review?).
_usage.step_5: Save as tests/fixtures/benchmark_queries_custom.json, register in evals/quality/runner.py SUITES, then run: neuralmind benchmark --quality
_usage.pro_tip: Start with 5 queries around your #1 bug area. If auth bugs happen most, write 5 auth queries — that's where ROI shows fastest.
